title: Goniophlebium articulatum
intro: Goniophlebium articulatum, also known as Articulated Bird's Nest Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can aid in wound healing and protection against cellular damage. The bioactive constituents of Goniophlebium articulatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Goniophlebium articulatum is often used to prepare herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Goniophlebium articulatum is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Goniophlebium, which is a group of ferns found in tropical regions. The family Polypodiaceae is a large and diverse group of ferns, with many species found in various parts of the world. The classification of Goniophlebium articulatum is further detailed under the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum Pteridophyta. The exact classification of this species is subject to ongoing research and may be refined in the future.

It is believed to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its therapeutic effects.
